channel.title: School

item.title: CheatSheet Study in Scarlet
item.description:
#crime

Arthur Conan Doyle, A Study in Scarlet
1st Sherlock Holmes novel, 1887
genre: #crime


With this small novel Doyle introduced the world to Sherlock Holmes. The series became a large success. Can you explain why?
The character of Watson is said to be based on Doyle himself. What do they have in common? (naval doctor)
The case they must solve takes its beginning in the States in a Mormon community. How relevant is this?
Comment on the structure of the text. (Large parts of it are recollections of life in the Mormon community in the US; so rather unusual as the crime story actually takes a back seat.
Crime stories – and this is one of the early ones in the genre – help cast an eye on social conditions and social problems not so often brought up in other literature. How can this be seen in this novel?
To what extent are the characters and their peculiarities introduced and detailed in this first Sherlock Holmes novel?
Arthur Conan Doyle, 1887 Relationship Types:
Blue solid: Professional partnership
Green solid: Friendship/alliance
Red dashed: Murder/victim
Purple dashed: Romance/love
Orange dashed: Revenge/pursuit
Gray solid: Investigation/law enforcement
Brown dashed: Religious authority Sherlock HolmesConsulting DetectiveBrilliant, eccentric, analyticalDeductive reasoning masterDr. John WatsonArmy surgeon (retired)Loyal, practical, narratorHolmes's biographerInspector LestradeScotland YardCompetent but conventionalSkeptical of Holmes's methodsInspector GregsonScotland YardLestrade's rivalEager for creditEnoch DrebberMurder victimCorrupt Mormon elderLucy's forced suitorJoseph StangersonDrebber's secretarySecond murder victimComplicit in Lucy's fateJefferson HopeCab driver/murdererLucy's true loveRevenge-driven hunterMrs. Hudson221B Baker Street landladyPatient, long-sufferingProvides domestic stabilityLucy FerrierAdopted daughterBeautiful, strong-willedVictim of Mormon polygamyJohn FerrierLucy's adoptive fatherIndependent farmerRefuses Mormon conversionBrigham YoungMormon Prophet/LeaderAuthoritarian rulerEnforces polygamous marriagesElder KemballMormon elderYoung's enforcerThreatens the Ferriers detective partnership consulting relationshipconsulting relationshipScotland Yard rivals landladytenant murdersmurdersinvestigates murderinvestigates murdercaptures/interviews adoptive father true love forced suitor forces marriagethreatens/exilesenforces Young's will seeks revenge againstauthorizes marriagesecretary/accomplicedies, motivating revengemurdered, motivating revenge Recompile
